mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 545i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 545i (N62B44) engine typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A blown engine can cost £9,000–£13,000 to replace. Valve stem seal replacement costs around £1,500–£3,500, while timing chain guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

The N62 V8 has a mixed reputation. It's 'a solid engine overall' that 'perform well', but it has well-documented weaknesses: valve stem seals that become brittle and leak, timing chain guides that fail between 80,000–100,000 miles, cooling system failures, and multiple oil leak points. With preventative maintenance, the engine can last ~320,000 km (200,000 miles).

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 545i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Valve stem seal failure — causing blue smoke on start-up and heavy oil consumption, (2) Timing chain guide failure — causing rattle and risk of catastrophic engine damage, (3) Cooling system failures — water pump, thermostat and coolant transfer pipe, and (4) Oil leaks from valve covers, timing covers, alternator bracket and oil filter housing.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 545i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £3,000–£6,000 545i, a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 65–180% of the car's value. For a clean example with good body and no rust, replacement may be worthwhile given the E60's strong following. However, if the car has other issues (air suspension, iDrive, transmission), evaluate total restoration costs before committing. For examples under £3,000, the replacement cost may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 545i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 545i uses the N62B44 engine family. Engine codes include N62B44A (2003–2004, 320–333 hp) and N62B44B (2004–2005, 333 hp). The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N62 valve stem seals, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — valve stem seals can be replaced without removing the engine using specialist tools. The repair typically costs £1,500–£3,500. Parts cost around £500, with labour making up the majority. BMW recommends using high-quality OEM seals from Elring Klinger. If caught early, this repair can restore the engine to full health and prevent fouled spark plugs and damaged catalytic converters.

eraMap.title: BMW 545i — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 4.4L V8 — valve stem seals, timing chain guides and cooling system are the main weak points

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 545i engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The N62's complex architecture and tight engine bay make it a significant job.

problems.title: Valve Stem Seal Failure
problems.symptoms: Blue smoke from exhaust on start-up (especially after idling), heavy oil consumption (1 litre per 1,000 miles or more), fouled spark plugs, failed emissions test
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — seals become brittle with age and heat
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (valve stem seal replacement using specialist tool kit)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if oil consumption becomes excessive

problems.recommendation: Valve stem seal failure is the most notorious issue on the N62 engine. Originally equipped seals become brittle over time, no longer sealing to the valve stem, leading to heavy oil consumption and blue smoke on start-up. This is a labour-intensive job requiring specialist tools to replace the seals without removing the camshafts. Parts cost around £500, but labour is significant. If you notice blue smoke or excessive oil consumption, have the seals inspected. Catching this early prevents fouled spark plugs, damaged catalytic converters, and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full valve stem seal guide → /failures/n62-valve-stem-seals/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on start-up, chain "slap" sound, excessive engine vibration, plastic particles in oil sump, metallic noise from front of engine
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–100,000 miles — guides become brittle and crack
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£8,500 (if chain jumps or snaps — full engine replacement)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The timing chain guides on the N62 4.4L engine are a common failure point. The plastic guides become brittle with age and heat, cracking and allowing the chain to become slack. Symptoms include excessive vibration, chain "slap," and plastic particles contaminating the oil. If left unattended, the timing chain can jump, causing pistons to hit valves and resulting in catastrophic engine damage. A timing chain service typically costs £1,500–£3,500, while a snapped chain requires a £5,500–£8,500 engine replacement. At the first sign of rattle, get the chain inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/n62-timing-chain-guides/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Cooling System Failures
problems.symptoms: Overheating, coolant loss, steam from engine bay, temperature gauge rising, heater blowing cold
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if temperature rises

problems.recommendation: The N62's cooling system is a known weak point. Common failures include the water pump (electric, known to fail without warning), thermostat, and the coolant transfer pipe seal. The rear coolant cover seal is also a documented failure point. Regular maintenance and timely replacement of these components are essential. If the temperature gauge rises, shut the engine down immediately — overheating an all-aluminium N62 can cause head gasket failure or worse. Preventative replacement of the water pump and thermostat at 60,000–80,000 miles is strongly recommended.

failureLink.label: Read the full cooling system guide

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Valve Cover, Timing Cover, Alternator Bracket)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, oil spots under the car, visible oil residue around the engine, low oil level warning
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (gasket replacement — valve cover, timing cover, alternator bracket)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are one of the most common complaints on the N62. The valve cover gaskets, upper timing cover gaskets, alternator bracket gasket, oil filter housing gaskets, and crankshaft oil seals are all known to leak. One owner reported that "too many points of failure" and "too many O rings" make the engine prone to leaks. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catching leaks early prevents oil contamination of other components and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/n62-oil-leaks/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 545i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with BMW-approved oil changes (5W-30, 8 litres)
* Evidence of recent timing chain guide replacement — this is critical
* Valve stem seals already replaced — this is the most notorious N62 issue
* Cooling system overhaul completed (water pump, thermostat, hoses)
avoidIf: * Blue smoke on start-up — indicates valve stem seal failure
* Rattling noise from the front of the engine — timing chain guide failure
* Overheating history or coolant system issues — the N62 is unforgiving of overheating
* No service history — V8 BMWs are expensive to maintain; lack of history is a red flag

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 545i's N62 V8 is a charismatic engine — a 4.4-litre naturally aspirated V8 with a glorious soundtrack and 333 hp. But it's also one of BMW's most complex and demanding engines. The N62 has a mixed reliability reputation: "a solid engine overall" that "perform well" but with well-documented weaknesses. Valve stem seal failure is the most notorious issue, followed by timing chain guide failure and cooling system problems. Buy a well-maintained example with documented preventative work — especially timing chain guides and valve stem seals — or budget for these repairs immediately. Without history? The repair bills can be eye-watering.

cta.label: → Compare 545i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 545i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (timing chain guides) and blue smoke (valve stem seals)
* Oil Consumption — Ask about oil usage; 1 litre per 1,000 miles suggests valve stem seal wear
* Service History — Including the N62B44 engine and confirmed BMW-approved oil changes
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain, guides and tensioners been replaced? This is critical
* Valve Stem Seals — Have they been replaced? This is the most notorious N62 issue
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks; ask about water pump and thermostat history
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve covers, timing covers and alternator bracket
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for VANOS, Valvetronic or misfire codes

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.value: DOHC, 4 valves per cylinder, chain-driven, Valvetronic, double VANOS
